title: CIVICUS Digital Action Lab Cohort 3 (2026): Grants of Up to $20,000 for Civil Society Organisations
summary: Applications are now open for the Digital Action Lab (DAL) Cohort 3 , an initiative by CIVICUS Global Alliance that supports civil society organisations to design, test, and scale innovative digital solutions that strengthen civic space, inclusive democracy, and citizen engagement. The programme is designed for organisations working in challenging or restricted civic environments and offers a combination of funding, technical support, and peer learning opportunities to help turn ideas into impactful digital action. About the Digital Action Lab The Digital Action Lab is a global programme that empowers civil society actors to harness digital tools to advance democratic participation and accountability. It focuses on strengthening the ability of organisations to operate effectively in complex civic and political contexts while using technology responsibly and strategically. Through a structured yet flexible model, the programme integrates learning, experimentation, and collaboration to support organisations from concept development to real-world implementation. What the Programme Offers Selected organisations will benefit from a comprehensive support package that includes: Grants ranging from USD 10,000 to USD 20,000 to implement digital action projects Technical and strategic support to strengthen digital skills and advocacy approaches Hands-on guidance on the safe and effective use of digital tools in challenging environments Opportunities for peer learning and collaboration with organisations across different regions Continuous mentorship and accompaniment throughout project implementation Access to global networks and knowledge-sharing platforms within the CIVICUS ecosystem Programme Duration and Structure The Digital Action Lab Cohort 3 runs for nine months and follows a cohort-based model designed to support organisations from application to impact. The programme includes the following stages: Call for Applications: An open call invites eligible civil society organisations to apply Selection: Up to ten organisations are selected through a competitive process Co-Design and Learning: Participants refine and strengthen their project ideas with support from experts and peers Funding: Selected organisations receive grants to implement their projects Implementation and Accompaniment: Ongoing technical support and coaching are provided throughout the implementation phase Peer Learning and Exchange: Participants share experiences, challenges, and solutions across regions Impact and Knowledge Sharing: Results, lessons learned, and best practices are documented and shared widely Eligibility Criteria The programme is open to: Civil society organisations, including formal and informal groups, networks, movements, and coalitions Individual activists who are formally affiliated with a civil society organisation Applicants must: Be based in OECD DAC ODA-eligible countries Be actively working on democracy and/or civic space issues Have some experience using digital tools for advocacy, organising, or service delivery Demonstrate the ability to manage grant funding and commit staff time for the full duration of the programme Be able to work and submit deliverables in English Priority Applicants Preference will be given to: Women-led organisations Youth-led initiatives LGBTQI+ groups Indigenous communities Grassroots organisations and underrepresented actors Organisations operating in obstructed or repressed civic space contexts Local offices of organisations based in the Global North are not eligible to apply. Why You Should Apply The Digital Action Lab provides more than financial support. It is a platform for innovation, collaboration, and capacity strengthening that enables organisations to: Develop practical digital solutions for civic engagement and democratic participation Strengthen their resilience and adaptability in restrictive environments Collaborate with peers and experts across regions Scale impactful ideas through structured support and funding Contribute to global knowledge on digital civic action and innovation Impact of the Digital Action Lab The programme has already supported multiple organisations globally, helping them turn innovative ideas into actionable projects. Through its approach, the Digital Action Lab contributes to strengthening civic participation and building more inclusive democratic systems. Apply here FAQs Find out more here Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the Digital Action Lab Cohort 3 is: 30 April 2026 How to Apply Interested organisations are required to complete the official application form and provide clear and concise information about their organisation and proposed project. Applicants should outline: Their organisational background The digital solution they wish to develop The problem they aim to address Expected outcomes and impact Their capacity to deliver the project Applications should be accurate, practical, and aligned with the objectives of the programme. Apply here FAQs Find out more here Pro Tip for Applicants Strong applications clearly demonstrate a well-defined problem, a practical and achievable digital solution, and a realistic plan for implementation within the nine-month timeframe. Applicants should also highlight how their project will create measurable impact and contribute to strengthening civic space. For More Opportunities For more global opportunities, including fellowships, internships, scholarships, and grants, visit: https://opportunitiesforyouth.org The post CIVICUS Digital Action Lab Cohort 3 (2026): Grants of Up to $20,000 for Civil Society Organisations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: NCD Alliance Consultancy Opportunity 2026: Exploring the Role of Private Philanthropy in Global Health Advocacy
summary: The global fight against noncommunicable diseases (NCDs) continues to gain urgency as millions of lives are impacted annually by conditions such as cancer, diabetes, cardiovascular diseases, chronic respiratory illnesses, and mental health disorders. At the forefront of this movement is the NCD Alliance (NCDA), a leading global civil society network committed to reducing preventable suffering and advancing health equity worldwide. Founded in 2009 and headquartered in Geneva, the NCD Alliance has grown into a powerful coalition of over 400 organizations spanning more than 80 countries. Through strategic advocacy, capacity building, and partnerships, the organization plays a pivotal role in shaping policies and mobilizing resources to address the global NCD burden. As part of its ongoing efforts to strengthen the global response to NCDs, the NCD Alliance is currently inviting applications for a consultancy under its Partnerships Memberships team. This opportunity centers on a critical and timely theme: the role of private philanthropy in advancing the NCD response globally. About the Consultancy Opportunity This consultancy is designed for experienced professionals or organizations capable of conducting in-depth research and producing actionable insights on how private philanthropic actors can contribute more effectively to the global NCD agenda. The selected consultant will be expected to develop a comprehensive project proposal that examines funding trends, identifies gaps, and proposes innovative strategies for leveraging private philanthropy in tackling NCDs. The consultant will work closely with the Partnerships Memberships team and report directly to the Director of Partnerships Memberships. This role offers a unique opportunity to contribute to high-level policy discussions and influence how philanthropic funding can be mobilized to support health systems, advocacy, and patient-centered initiatives. Key Responsibilities The selected consultant will: Conduct detailed research on private philanthropic contributions to NCD-related initiatives. Analyze existing funding mechanisms and identify opportunities for increased engagement. Develop a strategic report outlining recommendations for enhancing philanthropic involvement. Collaborate with NCDA stakeholders to ensure alignment with organizational priorities. Deliver high-quality written outputs, including reports and supporting documentation. Why Work with NCD Alliance? Joining the NCD Alliance as a consultant comes with several compelling advantages: Global Impact: Contribute to initiatives that directly influence global health outcomes and policies. Professional Network: Engage with leading advocates, policymakers, and health experts from around the world. Thought Leadership: Be part of a respected organization known for shaping the global NCD agenda. Flexible Engagement: As a consultancy role, this opportunity allows for flexibility while working on meaningful, high-impact projects. Application Process Interested applicants are required to submit the following documents: A detailed project proposal outlining the approach and methodology. A budget proposal. Curriculum Vitae (CV) or professional portfolio. A sample of a previously written resource or report. Interested consultants should send their project proposal and budget, together with their CV/portfolio and a sample written resource via email to James Bramble, Director of Partnerships and Membership, at jbramble@ncdalliance.org . Important Deadline All applications must be submitted on or before 12 April 2026. Early submissions are encouraged, as the position is expected to commence as soon as possible after the selection process.This consultancy represents more than just a professional opportunity;it is a chance to contribute to a global movement aimed at saving lives and improving health outcomes. By exploring the role of private philanthropy, the selected consultant will help unlock new pathways for funding and collaboration in the fight against NCDs. For professionals passionate about global health, policy, and sustainable development, this is a unique platform to make a lasting impact. Deadline : 12 April 2026 For more information about this opportunity, click here. Discover more global opportunities on OFY HERE. title: Ideas Fund Grant 2026: Funding for Innovative Nonprofits Driving Financial Inclusion, Fair Work, and Sustainability
summary: The Ideas Fund is now open to support nonprofit organizations with bold, innovative solutions that address some of today’s most pressing social and environmental challenges. This grant opportunity is designed to help organizations test new ideas, scale impactful initiatives, and drive meaningful change across communities. About the Ideas Fund The Ideas Fund focuses on supporting nonprofits that are developing creative and practical approaches in three key areas: Enhancing financial capability Promoting access to fair and decent work Strengthening the natural environment through sustainable solutions The goal is to back ideas that are not only innovative but also scalable, sustainable, and impactful . What the Fund Supports Selected organizations will receive funding to: Pilot new ideas and approaches Test innovative solutions in real-world settings Strengthen program impact and reach Develop sustainable models for long-term change Who Should Apply This opportunity is ideal for: Nonprofit organizations Social enterprises with a strong social mission Organizations working on economic inclusion, employment, or sustainability Teams with innovative ideas ready to be tested or scaled Why This Opportunity Matters Many organizations have powerful ideas but lack the funding to test or implement them. The Ideas Fund bridges that gap by providing the resources needed to turn bold concepts into real-world impact . If your organization is working to improve livelihoods, expand access to fair work, or protect the environment, this fund can help accelerate your impact. How to Apply Learn more and apply here. For more Grant opportunities , Visit OFY here. title: Aspen Religion Fellows 2026: $15,000 Fellowship for Emerging Leaders Advancing Pluralism
summary: The Aspen Institute has officially launched applications for its inaugural Aspen Religion Fellows Program , a prestigious opportunity designed for rising leaders working at the intersection of religion and society. This 18-month fellowship supports individuals committed to strengthening pluralism, dialogue, and collaboration across diverse communities. About the Fellowship The Aspen Religion Fellows Program brings together a cohort of 10 leaders aged 25–40 from across multiple sectors to explore how religion intersects with public life and global challenges. Fellows will participate in a series of immersive convenings, including: A week-long session at the Aspen Ideas Festival A three-day gathering in Chicago , United States A convening in the nation’s capital ( Washington, D.C. ) Fellowship Focus Areas Applicants can come from five key sectors: Religion Clerical Leadership Religion Climate Religion Media Religion Nonprofit Leadership Religion Social Trust Benefits $15,000 stipend to fund a self-designed pluralism project Access to a powerful network of advisors, mentors, and fellows Participation in high-level convenings and leadership development sessions Opportunities to strengthen dialogue, collaboration, and leadership skills Who Should Apply This fellowship is ideal for: Leaders aged 25–40 Individuals working across religion, media, climate, nonprofits, and social impact Changemakers interested in promoting pluralism and social cohesion Professionals seeking to expand their leadership and influence Why This Fellowship Matters In a world increasingly shaped by division, this fellowship equips leaders with the tools to engage across differences, foster understanding, and build stronger communities . It’s not just about leadership, it’s about learning how to listen, collaborate, and lead with empathy in complex environments. Application Details Applications opened on April 2, 2026 . 🔗 Learn more and apply For more Fellowship Opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: Mother Cabrini Health Foundation 2026 Grants: Funding Opportunity for Health-Focused Organizations in New York
summary: The Mother Cabrini Health Foundation has officially opened its 2026 grant application process , inviting organizations to submit a Letter of Inquiry (LOI) for funding support. This initiative is focused on advancing the health and well-being of vulnerable communities across New York , providing funding to organizations working on impactful, community-driven solutions. About the Grant Opportunity Organizations can apply under five key program areas: Access to Healthcare Basic Needs Healthcare Workforce Mental and Behavioral Health General Fund Applicants are encouraged to choose the program that best aligns with their mission and project goals. Who Can Apply Eligible applicants include organizations that: Align with the foundation’s mission to improve health outcomes Serve vulnerable populations in New York Have projects focused on healthcare access, workforce, or community well-being Current grantees seeking renewal funding must also apply through this LOI process. Application Timeline LOI Submission Window: March 30 – April 23, 2026 Applicant Webinar: April 7, 2026 Full Proposal Invitations: Mid-June 2026 Grant Decisions: October and December 2026 Why This Opportunity Matters This grant programme provides critical funding for organizations addressing pressing health challenges, from improving access to care to supporting mental health and workforce development. For nonprofits and social impact organizations working in New York, this is a valuable opportunity to secure funding, scale impact, and reach more communities in need. title: CERN Studentships 2026 (Technical, Administrative & Doctoral) – Paid Opportunities in Switzerland
summary: Key Details Host: CERN Location: Geneva, Switzerland Programmes: Technical, Administrative, and Doctoral Studentships Deadline: 30 June 2026 Duration: 2–12 months Funding: 3,486–3,900 CHF per month (tax-free) About the CERN Studentships The CERN Studentship Programme 2026 provides students and doctoral candidates with the opportunity to gain hands-on experience in a world-leading scientific environment. Participants work on real projects in engineering, IT, administration, and applied sciences while contributing to cutting-edge research. Available Programmes Technical Studentship (engineering, IT, applied sciences) Administrative Studentship (HR, finance, law, communications, business) Doctoral Studentship (PhD research aligned with CERN projects) Benefits Monthly allowance of 3,486–3,900 CHF (tax-free) Comprehensive health insurance Travel expenses support (conditions apply) Family allowance (where applicable) Up to 8 weeks of paid leave per year Opportunity to work in an international research environment Eligibility Criteria Must be a national of a CERN Member or Associated Member State Must have completed at least 18 months of study (minimum 3 semesters) or be about to start a PhD Must remain enrolled as a full-time student during the programme Must have a strong academic background in a relevant field Full Member States Austria Belgium Bulgaria Czech Republic Denmark Finland France Germany Greece Hungary Israel Italy Netherlands Norway Poland Portugal Romania Serbia Slovakia Spain Sweden Switzerland United Kingdom Associate Member States (Eligible for many CERN programmes, including studentships) Albania Armenia Brazil Cyprus Estonia Georgia India Latvia Lithuania Morocco Pakistan Slovenia Thailand Türkiye Ukraine Key Note for Applicants Most CERN studentship programmes are open to nationals of both Member and Associate Member States . If your country is listed above, you are generally eligible to apply (subject to programme-specific requirements). Why Apply Working at CERN provides direct exposure to advanced technologies and global research collaborations. This experience strengthens both academic and professional career pathways. Find out more and Apply now: Technical Studentship: https://careers.cern/programmes/technical-studentship/ Administrative Studentship: https://careers.cern/programmes/administrative-studentship/ Doctoral Studentship: https://careers.cern/programmes/doctoral-studentship/ More about CERN visit: https://careers.cern/cern/ Pro Tip Emphasize practical experience, technical skills, and research alignment with CERN’s work in your application Find out more and Apply now: Technical Studentship: https://careers.cern/programmes/technical-studentship/ Administrative Studentship: https://careers.cern/programmes/administrative-studentship/ Doctoral Studentship: https://careers.cern/programmes/doctoral-studentship/ More about CERN visit: https://careers.cern/cern/ The post CERN Studentships 2026 (Technical, Administrative Doctoral) – Paid Opportunities in Switzerland appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Immigrant Justice Corps 2026 Community Fellowship: Fully Funded 3-Year Opportunity for Aspiring Immigration Advocates
summary: The 2026 Community Fellowship by Immigrant Justice Corps (IJC) stands as one of the most impactful and prestigious opportunities for young professionals passionate about immigration justice, social equity, and legal advocacy in the United States. As the nation’s first and only fellowship program dedicated exclusively to immigration, IJC continues to bridge the justice gap by placing talented graduates into leading legal service organizations. With applications now open for its twelfth cohort, this fully funded, three-year fellowship offers a transformative career pathway for individuals committed to supporting low-income immigrant communities while gaining hands-on legal experience. What is the IJC Community Fellowship? The IJC Community Fellowship is a structured, paid program designed to recruit recent college graduates and immerse them in frontline immigration advocacy. Fellows are placed within highly respected legal service organizations, where they directly support underserved immigrant populations. Currently, IJC hosts over 130 fellows across more than 30 U.S. states, contributing significantly to expanding access to justice. The 2026 cohort introduces a unique opportunity with three-year placements , allowing fellows to develop expertise, build professional networks, and achieve formal accreditation in immigration legal practice. Fellowship Structure and Professional Development The Community Fellowship is carefully designed to provide both practical experience and professional certification. Accreditation Pathway Fellows are expected to progress through the U.S. Department of Justice Recognition Accreditation Program: Partial Accreditation : Achieved within the first year (ideally within 6–12 months), enabling fellows to represent clients before U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services. Full Accreditation : Targeted between 12–18 months, allowing fellows to practice before Immigration Courts. This structured pathway ensures that fellows graduate with strong legal competencies and recognized credentials. Key Responsibilities of Community Fellows Participants in the program take on meaningful and impactful roles, including: Managing individual caseloads under legal supervision Providing direct legal services and representation to immigrants Translating complex immigration policies into accessible language Supporting communities through culturally competent advocacy Engaging with diverse populations facing legal and systemic barriers This immersive experience equips fellows with both technical legal skills and deep community engagement expertise. Fellowship Benefits The IJC Community Fellowship offers a comprehensive benefits package, making it one of the most competitive programs in the field: Full-time salary Employee benefits Extensive training and mentorship Hands-on legal experience Professional accreditation pathway Long-term career development in immigration law and advocacy Placement Locations For the 2026 cohort, placements are expected to be based in: New York City Long Island Upstate New York Fellows must be willing to relocate to their assigned location. While some placements may offer hybrid work arrangements, fully remote participation is not available. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be current undergraduate students graduating by Spring 2026 or recent graduates (no earlier than Spring 2024) Hold an Associate or Bachelor’s degree Be eligible for work authorization in the United States for the full duration of the fellowship Demonstrate proficiency in English and at least one additional language Spanish (including indigenous dialects) is strongly preferred Applicants with DACA or TPS status are encouraged to apply. Application Components Candidates must submit a complete application package including: Completed application form Resume or CV (maximum two pages) Unofficial academic transcripts Statement of Interest (maximum 500 words) Essay on immigration policy (maximum 500 words) At least one letter of recommendation (two recommended) The written components are critical and should reflect the applicant’s passion, cultural awareness, and commitment to immigration justice. Selection Process The selection process is competitive and takes place in multiple stages: Application Review (Early May 2026) Interviews (Mid-May 2026, virtual format) Final Matching Process (Late May 2026) Fellowship Agreements Issued (June 2026) Successful applicants are matched with host organizations based on mutual preferences and program needs. Application Deadline All application materials, including recommendation letters, must be submitted by: Sunday, May 3, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. (ET) Applicants are strongly encouraged to begin early and maintain close communication with their referees to ensure timely submission. Why Apply for the IJC Community Fellowship? This fellowship is more than a professional opportunity—it is a platform to create meaningful social impact. Fellows become part of a national movement working to ensure equitable access to justice for immigrant communities. By combining legal training, community engagement, and professional mentorship, the IJC Community Fellowship prepares participants for long-term careers in law, policy, and advocacy. For more information click here APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post Immigrant Justice Corps 2026 Community Fellowship: Fully Funded 3-Year Opportunity for Aspiring Immigration Advocates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: Paid Remote Climate & Conservation Finance Internship 2026 with The Nature Conservancy
summary: Are you looking to gain hands-on experience in climate finance, policy, and global sustainability? The Climate Conservation Finance Policy Internship 2026 with The Nature Conservancy (TNC) is a paid, fully remote opportunity designed for students and recent graduates eager to work at the intersection of finance, policy, and environmental impact. Over eight weeks, interns will contribute to real-world projects, support global initiatives, and build valuable professional skills. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is strongly encouraged. About the Opportunity The Nature Conservancy (TNC) is one of the world’s leading environmental organizations, working to protect lands and waters globally. Through its Internships @TNC program , the organization offers structured, paid opportunities for emerging professionals to apply their academic knowledge to real-world conservation challenges. This specific internship is part of the Climate and Conservation Finance Policy (CCFP) team , which focuses on financing climate and nature solutions in emerging markets and developing economies. The team works with governments, financial institutions, and global stakeholders to design and implement innovative financial mechanisms that support sustainability and economic resilience. Interns in this role will contribute to projects that explore tools such as debt-for-nature swaps, green finance frameworks, ecosystem service payments, and climate-related financial strategies . They will also support research and policy development aimed at integrating nature and biodiversity into economic systems and financial decision-making. The internship runs for 8 weeks, from 15 June to 7 August 2026 , and is conducted remotely. Interns will participate in onboarding, training, mentorship, and networking activities, while also completing a capstone project that showcases their learning and contributions. Why This Opportunity Matters Climate change and environmental degradation are among the most pressing global challenges today. Addressing these issues requires innovative financial solutions that can support sustainable development while protecting natural ecosystems. The work of the CCFP team at The Nature Conservancy is critical in advancing these solutions by helping governments and institutions design financial systems that integrate environmental priorities. This internship allows participants to contribute to efforts that have real-world policy and economic implications , particularly in developing regions where the need for sustainable solutions is urgent. By joining this program, interns gain insight into how global environmental strategies are developed and implemented, while also building the skills needed to contribute to long-term sustainability goals. What You’ll Gain This internship offers a strong foundation for careers in sustainability, policy, and international development: Paid Experience: Earn $22 per hour while gaining professional experience in a global organization. Real-World Impact: Work on projects that influence climate and conservation finance strategies worldwide. Skill Development: Build expertise in research, policy writing, data analysis, and stakeholder engagement. Mentorship and Training: Receive guidance from experienced professionals and participate in structured learning sessions. Global Exposure: Contribute to international initiatives and support major events such as climate and biodiversity forums. This opportunity is particularly valuable for individuals aiming to enter careers in climate policy, development finance, or environmental economics . Eligibility To be eligible for this internship, applicants must: Be currently enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate program , or be a recent graduate (within six months) Have a background in policy, finance, economics, environmental studies, or related fields Demonstrate experience in research, writing, and communication Be fluent in English Preferred qualifications include: Experience in climate finance, international development, or public policy Familiarity with data analysis or visualization tools Ability to work independently in a remote and collaborative environment Knowledge of additional languages such as Spanish, Portuguese, or French Application Process and Deadline To apply for the Climate Conservation Finance Policy Internship: Prepare Your Application: Update your CV and ensure your experience aligns with the role requirements. Apply Online: Submit your application through The Nature Conservancy’s official careers portal. Rolling Review: Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and the position may close once a strong candidate pool is identified. Internship Duration: 15 June – 7 August 2026 Location: Remote Compensation: $22/hour To learn more and apply, visit the official Nature Conservancy internship job posting . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Susan Carroll Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Investigative Journalism Opportunity for Emerging Reporters
summary: The Susan Carroll Fellowship, offered by the Investigative Reporters and Editors (IRE), is a prestigious and highly competitive opportunity designed to empower emerging journalists with the skills, mentorship, and financial support needed to excel in investigative reporting. Named in honor of the late investigative journalist Susan Carroll, this fellowship aims to continue her legacy of impactful, data-driven journalism that holds institutions accountable and amplifies underreported stories. This fellowship is particularly significant in today’s media landscape, where investigative journalism plays a crucial role in uncovering truths, promoting transparency, and driving societal change. The program is structured to support early-career journalists by providing them with access to training, resources, and a network of experienced professionals in the field. Overview of the Susan Carroll Fellowship The Susan Carroll Fellowship is designed for journalists who are passionate about investigative reporting but may lack access to the resources and training needed to fully develop their potential. Through this fellowship, selected participants receive financial assistance to attend IRE’s renowned training programs, where they can gain hands-on experience and learn advanced investigative techniques. The fellowship places a strong emphasis on data journalism, a field that Susan Carroll was deeply committed to. Participants are trained to analyze complex datasets, uncover hidden patterns, and transform raw information into compelling stories that inform and engage the public. What the Fellowship Offers One of the most attractive aspects of the Susan Carroll Fellowship is its comprehensive support package. Fellows receive funding that covers the cost of attending IRE training events, including registration fees, travel expenses, and accommodation. This ensures that financial constraints do not hinder talented journalists from accessing world-class training. In addition to financial support, fellows gain access to: Intensive investigative journalism training sessions Workshops on data analysis, reporting techniques, and storytelling Networking opportunities with leading journalists and media professionals Mentorship from experienced investigative reporters Exposure to cutting-edge tools and technologies used in modern journalism These benefits collectively create a transformative learning experience that equips fellows with the skills needed to produce impactful investigative stories. Training and Learning Experience The fellowship provides an immersive learning environment where participants engage in practical, hands-on training. Workshops are led by industry experts who share real-world insights and best practices. Topics typically include: Data collection and cleaning Statistical analysis and visualization Freedom of information requests Ethical considerations in investigative journalism Digital security for journalists By the end of the program, fellows are expected to have a deeper understanding of investigative reporting processes and the confidence to undertake complex journalistic projects. Application Process and Deadline Applicants are required to submit a detailed application that demonstrates their interest in investigative journalism, their career goals, and how the fellowship will help them achieve those goals. Supporting materials may include writing samples, a resume, and references. Deadline: The application deadline varies annually. Applicants are strongly encouraged to check the official fellowship page and apply early to avoid missing out on this opportunity. Why You Should Apply The Susan Carroll Fellowship is more than just a training program; it is a career-defining opportunity for aspiring investigative journalists. It not only enhances technical skills but also provides a platform for fellows to connect with a global community of professionals who are dedicated to making a difference through journalism. For early-career journalists, this fellowship can serve as a stepping stone to greater opportunities, including positions in leading media organizations, collaborations on high-impact investigations, and recognition within the journalism community.Moreover, the fellowship’s focus on inclusivity ensures that journalists from diverse backgrounds have the opportunity to participate and contribute their unique perspectives to the field.In an era where credible information is more important than ever, initiatives like the Susan Carroll Fellowship play a vital role in strengthening the field of investigative journalism. By investing in the next generation of reporters, the fellowship helps ensure that important stories continue to be told and that accountability remains a cornerstone of society. If you are passionate about uncovering the truth and making a meaningful impact through journalism, the Susan Carroll Fellowship is an opportunity you should not overlook. Deadline: Varies annually (apply early) APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. For more fellowship opportunities, click here . title: Empowering Women for Climate Action: Sustainable Livelihoods and Community Resilience.
summary: Across the globe, the intersection of climate change and gender inequality continues to shape the lives of millions. Addressing these challenges requires innovative, inclusive, and community-driven solutions. At the forefront of this movement is SCWEI, an organization committed to empowering women while advancing sustainable climate action. About the Initiative SCWEI is dedicated to promoting sustainable climate solutions while uplifting underprivileged women. By integrating environmental action with economic empowerment, the organization is creating pathways for women to lead, thrive, and contribute meaningfully to their communities. Mission “To empower underprivileged women as leaders in climate resilience and sustainability, equipping them with essential skills and resources for dignified livelihoods and economic security. Through our initiatives, we bridge environmental action with economic opportunity, creating communities that thrive in harmony with nature and equality.” Vision “A sustainable world led by empowered women, where communities protect the environment and achieve lasting economic security, creating a future of equal opportunity, ecological balance, and shared prosperity.” Launch of the Community-Based Female Tailoring Training Center As part of its commitment to women’s empowerment, SCWEI has launched a Community-Based Female Tailoring Training Center. This initiative is designed to equip women with practical, income-generating skills that foster independence and long-term economic stability. The center recently hosted its first training session, attracting a strong and enthusiastic group of participants. The session was marked by a spirit of collaboration, determination, and hope, as women began their journey toward self-reliance and financial empowerment. This milestone reflects SCWEI’s broader goal of creating inclusive opportunities where women can transform their skills into sustainable livelihoods. By supporting women at the grassroots level, the organization is strengthening community resilience and promoting economic inclusion. Driving Sustainable Climate and Women Empowerment SCWEI’s work goes beyond skills training. The organization is deeply committed to advancing sustainable climate practices while empowering women through: Education and Capacity Building: Providing women with knowledge and tools to adapt to climate challenges. Advocacy: Promoting gender equality and environmental sustainability at local and global levels. Community Initiatives: Implementing projects that enhance climate resilience and improve livelihoods. These efforts contribute to building communities that are both environmentally sustainable and socially equitable. Join the Movement Climate change and gender inequality are interconnected challenges that require collective action. SCWEI invites individuals, partners, and communities to join hands in addressing these pressing issues. Support initiatives that empower women through skills and education Advocate for climate resilience and sustainable practices Participate in programs that drive community transformation Together, we can create a future where women lead climate action, communities thrive sustainably, and opportunities are accessible to all. Click Here to get involved. Empowering women is not just a social priority—it is a critical strategy for achieving sustainable development and climate resilience. Through initiatives like the Community-Based Female Tailoring Training Center, SCWEI is demonstrating how targeted interventions can transform lives and strengthen communities. By bridging the gap between environmental sustainability and economic opportunity, the organization is paving the way for a more inclusive, balanced, and prosperous world. Discover more similar opportunities Here. The post Empowering Women for Climate Action: Sustainable Livelihoods and Community Resilience. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
